title: Thyella lactescens
taxonomy: Thyella lactescens is a species of sea snail, specifically a marine gastropod mollusk in the family Lottiidae. It belongs to the genus Thyella, which is a group of sea snails that are primarily found in the Indo-Pacific region. This species is further classified under the kingdom Animalia, phylum Mollusca, and class Gastropoda. Thyella lactescens is a type of top shell that is characterized by its unique shell shape and coloration. The species is also known to have a distinctive white or yellowish shell with brown or dark brown markings.

intro: Thyella lactescens, also known as milk flower, is a flowering plant species within the family Fabaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in soothing digestive issues and boosting the immune system. The bioactive constituents of Thyella lactescens include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
